title: leptoderris fasciculata
intro: Leptoderris fasciculata, also known as the fascicled derris, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is a woody vine that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant contains rotenone, a toxic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which makes it useful for relieving pain and reducing swelling. Leptoderris fasciculata can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide various health benefits.
